implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: record_identity(natural_key: str) -> uuid.UUID function with deterministic UUIDv5 generation
content_hash(payload: BasePayload) -> str function with version-invariant hashing
Pure functions with zero I/O dependencies

completion_promises.criterion_text: `record_identity` returns a UUIDv5; the same natural key yields a byte-identical UUID on repeated calls and across separate processes
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented record_identity() using uuid.uuid5() with FLEET_MEMORY_NAMESPACE. Tests verify: (1) result is UUIDv5, (2) same key produces identical UUID across multiple calls, (3) byte-level identity confirmed via uuid.bytes comparison.

completion_promises.criterion_text: The UUID namespace constant is a single module-level constant reused by every call — no per-call or per-type namespace
completion_promises.evidence: Defined FLEET_MEMORY_NAMESPACE as a single module-level uuid.UUID constant. Test verifies record_identity() uses this constant by comparing output with manual uuid.uuid5(FLEET_MEMORY_NAMESPACE, key) computation.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Two payloads with the same `payload_type`/`project`/`identifier` resolve to the same identity; any differing segment resolves to a different identity
completion_promises.evidence: Tests verify identity uniqueness across all three segments: (1) same segments → same UUID, (2) different project → different UUID, (3) different identifier → different UUID, (4) different payload_type → different UUID.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `content_hash` is identical for two payloads whose semantic content is equal but whose `version` differs
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented content_hash() to exclude 'version' field from hash computation by filtering it out from model_dump(). Test creates two payloads with identical content but version=1 vs version=2, verifies hashes are identical.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `content_hash` differs when any byte of semantic content differs, including a single-character change
completion_promises.evidence: Tests verify hash sensitivity: (1) single-character source_ref change produces different hash, (2) different domain_tags produce different hash, (3) different supersedes list produces different hash, (4) completely different semantic content produces different hash.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Both functions are pure: no network, no database, no filesystem, no LLM import — verifiable by inspection and by the zero-LLM negative test
completion_promises.evidence: Module uses only uuid, hashlib, json, and BasePayload type import. Tests verify: (1) no forbidden imports (httpx, Settings, Store, openai, anthropic), (2) record_identity produces identical results across 10 calls (pure), (3) content_hash produces identical results across 10 calls (pure). Implementation contains zero I/O operations.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran 'ruff check src/fleet_memory/writer/' → All checks passed. Ran 'ruff format --check src/fleet_memory/writer/' → 2 files already formatted. Zero linting or formatting errors.
